quickAnswer: To handle creating a weekly meal plan and grocery list, Start with PDFs, decide that the output must be accurate study notes, use Gemini for one reviewable version, and check it before changing formats or adding another tool. Gemini should create the first reviewable version, Microsoft Copilot belongs in the second pass only when accurate study notes needs more control, and Todoist AI should be saved for review or export after the core result is right.
quickDecision: For creating a weekly meal plan and grocery list, choose Gemini first when the input is PDFs and the output needs to be accurate study notes. Choose Microsoft Copilot when source document matters more. Choose Todoist AI when you need a lighter review, export, or fallback pass.

steps.title: Prepare PDFs
steps.detail: Put the real input in one place before opening a tool: PDFs, lecture slides, class notes, research sources, creating a weekly meal plan and grocery list, any required facts, the audience, and the format you need at the end. Remove private details you are not allowed to share.
steps.why: AI output is only as useful as the material and constraints you give it.
steps.output: A clean input bundle and one clear target format: accurate study notes.

steps.title: Ask for the first usable version
steps.detail: Use Gemini for a first pass that tries to create a weekly meal plan and grocery list. Ask it to preserve source facts, flag uncertainty, and organize the result into sections you can review.
steps.why: The first pass should create a reviewable accurate study notes, not a finished answer you trust blindly.
steps.output: A structured accurate study notes with visible gaps or uncertain points.

steps.title: Check the answer before polishing
steps.detail: Compare the AI output with PDFs. Fix names, dates, numbers, unsupported claims, missing context, and tone before you ask for nicer wording.
steps.why: Polished wrong information is harder to catch than rough information.
steps.output: A corrected accurate study notes that is ready for formatting or wording cleanup.

steps.title: Change the format only after the facts are right
steps.detail: Use Microsoft Copilot when the verified accurate study notes needs a different format, structure, or source-aware second pass. Keep the same facts from PDFs so the rewrite does not drift from the original.
steps.why: Format changes are safer after the substance is already checked.
steps.output: A version that fits the channel, document, post, or recipient.

steps.title: Finish with a human review
steps.detail: Use Todoist AI for cleanup only after the main accurate study notes is checked against PDFs. Read the final result as the recipient would: on the device, in the app, or in the document where it will actually be used.
steps.why: The final review catches tone, missing context, and practical usability issues.
steps.output: A final result you can send, post, study from, or save with confidence.

keyTakeaways: What you need: PDFs, lecture slides, class notes, a clear output format, and a few minutes to review the result.
Start with Gemini for the first pass, but judge the output against creating a weekly meal plan and grocery list, not against tool hype.
Use Microsoft Copilot only when the workflow needs a different format or second pass.
Use Todoist AI for review after the main output is fact-checked.
The reader needs a realistic plan that fits time, budget, preferences, and pantry items.
Most tool lists skip constraints like prep time, leftovers, and grocery trips.
The useful next step is to test one real example before building a repeatable workflow.

bestPicksBySituation.why: Todoist AI helps when you need workflows that need task planning and prioritization and want a fallback that is easier to adapt on a phone or desktop.
